title: Add warning-only secret hygiene validation
description:
Created from the project state audit.

Add conservative warning-only validation for obvious secret-like content in Pinake Markdown documents.

Scope:
- Scan `.pinake/docs/**/*.md` for high-confidence token, private-key, password, and credential patterns.
- Keep findings warning-only to avoid blocking legitimate documentation workflows.
- Ignore safe placeholder names such as `EXAMPLE_API_TOKEN` and documented variable names without values.
- Include line numbers and clear suggestions in the output report and diagnostics if diagnostics are available.
- Document the behavior in security/privacy guidance and README.

Why this matters:
- The project already documents local-first security and secret-handling guidance, but validation does not help catch accidental sensitive content before commit.

Code anchors: `src/services/ValidationService.ts:217`, `docs/security-privacy.md:1`.

checklist.text: Define high-confidence secret-like patterns and safe placeholder exemptions
Add warning-only Markdown secret hygiene validation with line numbers
Add tests for true positives, placeholders, and false-positive control
Document the validation behavior in security/privacy guidance and README
